channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A Bible passage is at the center of a debate over how the U.S. should treat immigrants
item.description: A debate over the Bible verse Matthew 25 is pitting mainline pastors, Black protestants and the pope against evangelical politicians put on the defensive over President Trump's policies.
item.content:encoded: A debate over the Bible verse Matthew 25 is pitting mainline pastors, Black protestants and the pope against evangelical politicians put on the defensive over President Trump's policies.
item.dc:creator: Jack Jenkins

item.title: Could fish be a secret ingredient for rice farmers to avoid disease and make more money?
item.description: Farmers in Senegal are welcoming fish into their rice paddies. The hope is they'll fertilize the crop, be a source of food ... and eat the snails that carry parasitic worms.
item.content:encoded:
Farmers in Senegal are welcoming fish into their rice paddies. The hope is they'll fertilize the crop, be a source of food ... and eat the snails that carry parasitic worms.

(Image credit: Ricci Shyrock for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Getting campaign text messages ahead of midterms? There could be an AI bot behind it
item.description: Taught to sound like a candidate, bots are engaging voters with personalized text messages making AI-generated texting conversations the latest tool political campaigns are using to connect.
item.content:encoded:
Taught to sound like a candidate, bots are engaging voters with personalized text messages making AI-generated texting conversations the latest tool political campaigns are using to connect.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Maham Javaid

item.title: An artist brought 'I.C.E. pops' to a Texas campus. The show was shut down in days
item.description: The Trump administration's executive orders have meant that administrators are questioning what art can — and can't — be seen on campus.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration's executive orders have meant that administrators are questioning what art can — and can't — be seen on campus.

(Image credit: Laila AnnMarie Stevens for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: What a Monopoly importer learned when it tried to make things in the U.S.A.
item.description: After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.
item.content:encoded:
After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.

(Image credit: WS Game Company)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina, Trump ally and foreign policy hawk, dies at 71
item.description: His office said Graham died Saturday night after a "brief and sudden illness." Graham was instrumental in enacting Trump's policy and staffing priorities in the Senate.
item.content:encoded:
His office said Graham died Saturday night after a "brief and sudden illness." Graham was instrumental in enacting Trump's policy and staffing priorities in the Senate.

(Image credit: Jeffrey Collins)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Alvarez's 112th-minute goal helps lift Argentina past Switzerland 3-1 and into World Cup semifinals
item.description: Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.
item.content:encoded:
Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.

(Image credit: Abbie Parr)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Messi and Argentina survive another close call to reach the World Cup semifinals
item.description: Argentina was taken to the brink in its first two knockout games. In Saturday's quarterfinal against Switzerland, the Albiceleste survived again to advance to the semis, where they will face England.
item.content:encoded:
Argentina was taken to the brink in its first two knockout games. In Saturday's quarterfinal against Switzerland, the Albiceleste survived again to advance to the semis, where they will face England.

(Image credit: Odd Andersen)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: 200 young campers rescued as flooding hits parts of Missouri and Kentucky
item.description: A historic rainfall event has left communities across several Missouri counties underwater and prompted water rescues, including an evacuation of a summer camp.
item.content:encoded: A historic rainfall event has left communities across several Missouri counties underwater and prompted water rescues, including an evacuation of a summer camp.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: US attacks Iran over ship being hit in Strait of Hormuz; Tehran lashes out again at Gulf Arab states
item.description: The United States attacked Iran early Sunday morning over an Iranian attack on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran apparently responded with strikes targeting Bahrain, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates.
item.content:encoded:
The United States attacked Iran early Sunday morning over an Iranian attack on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran apparently responded with strikes targeting Bahrain, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
